# cipher-brain

> Encrypt a growing second-brain snapshot (gbrain, Claude Code memory, an Obsidian
> vault, a ChatGPT export) client-side with age (X25519 + ChaCha20-Poly1305), then
> park the ciphertext permanently on Arweave. Storage backends only ever see
> ciphertext. Recovery needs just the identity file and a locator — no wallet, no
> npm package, to pull the bytes back.

## Quickstart

```sh
npx cipher-brain init   # interactive wizard: keygen -> backup key -> first snapshot+push -> recovery kit
```

`init` requires stdin to be a TTY and refuses to run non-interactively (e.g. an AI
agent's shell). For non-interactive/scripted setup, drive the individual commands
it wraps (keygen, snapshot, push, schedule) by hand — see README.md's "Manual flow".

## Key concepts

- **identity** (private key) — the only thing that can decrypt. Lives off the
  always-on machine; lose it and the snapshots are unrecoverable.
- **recipient** (public key) — all the snapshotting machine needs. Safe to keep
  on the box that runs gbrain.
- **backend** — pluggable `push`/`pull` storage behind one interface: `file`
  (local, dev/CI), `turbo` (recommended — Arweave via a bundler, ETH/USDC),
  `arweave` (raw L1, small artifacts only).
- **recovery kit** — the printable artifact `init` produces: the backup identity
  (raw, not passphrase-wrapped — keep the kit itself physically secure), the
  latest locator, and restore steps. Kit-only recovery on a fresh machine needs
  both the backup key (`init` defaults to generating one, but it can be
  declined) and a portable backend (`turbo`/`arweave` — `file` locators are
  machine-local).

## Everyday commands

```sh
cipher-brain keygen                                             # one-time identity + recipient
cipher-brain snapshot --dir <path> --recipient <file> --out <f.age>
cipher-brain verify --in <f.age>                                # real ciphertext? wrong key rejected?
cipher-brain push --in <f.age> --backend turbo --yes             # pays a one-time bundler fee
cipher-brain pull --locator <id> --backend turbo --out <f.age>   # plain gateway fetch, no wallet
cipher-brain restore --in <f.age> --out-dir <dir>
cipher-brain schedule install --backend turbo --dir <path> --max-spend <n>   # unattended nightly
```

## Docs

- [README.md](README.md) — full walkthrough, threat model, backends, MCP server.
- [MANAGEMENT.md](MANAGEMENT.md) — cadence, versioning, restore runbook, key recovery.
- [docs/durability.md](docs/durability.md) — why Arweave is the recommended backend.
- [docs/arweave-upload-runbook.md](docs/arweave-upload-runbook.md) — funding an
  Arweave upload with ETH/USDC.
